images.caption: Figure 1:Pearson correlations of base models and human decisions (x-axis) vs. aligned models and human decisions (y-axis) across four game families. Each point is a same-provider pair evaluated in its native format (standard prompt for base, chat template for aligned).Points below the diagonal indicate base advantage.The shaded region marks pairs where both models correlate below 0.3 with human behavior. Base models win 75:4 in bargaining, 32:4 in persuasion, 25:1 in negotiation, and 81:13 in matrix games, for an overall ratio of 9.7:1 (213 vs. 22,p<10−40p<10^{-40}).

Figure 2:Median Pearson correlation difference (base minus aligned) by model size, with 95% bootstrap confidence intervals (5,000 resamples, percentile method). The base advantage is positive across all size bins and grows with scale.
